name: sample_0001
English_Report: Increased translucency is observed in the upper lobes of both lungs, with scattered cystic translucent shadows. Scattered small nodular shadows are present in both lungs, measuring approximately 3-6mm in diameter; some exhibit ground-glass density. The largest is located in the lateral basal segment of the right lower lobe (IM145). Punctate calcifications are seen in the upper lobes of both lungs. A few patchy shadows are noted in the right lower lobe. The trachea and bronchi are patent. No obvious enlarged lymph nodes are seen in the hilar or mediastinal regions of both lungs. The size and shape of the heart are normal, with no abnormalities in the pericardium. Dense shadows are observed in the coronary artery course area and the aortic wall. No pleural effusion is detected on either side. Additionally: A nodular dense shadow is seen in the left thyroid gland.

name: sample_0002
English_Report: Both lungs show good transparency. Tiny nodular shadows are seen in the right upper lobe and left lung, measuring approximately 3-4 mm in diameter. A few linear shadows are observed in the right middle and lower lobes. The trachea and bronchi are patent. No obvious enlarged lymph nodes are seen in the hilar or mediastinal regions on plain scan. The heart is normal in size and shape, and the pericardium is unremarkable. No pleural effusion is seen on either side. Multiple cystic low-density lesions are observed in both kidneys, with a few nodular dense shadows around some of them; the largest measures approximately 35 x 38 mm. Multiple nodular dense shadows are seen in the left renal sinus. The liver, spleen, pancreas, adrenal glands, prostate, and seminal vesicles are normal in size and shape, with no obvious abnormal density on plain scan. No significant wall thickening, mass, or stones are seen in the gallbladder or bile ducts, and no abnormal dilatation is noted. The bladder is poorly filled, and no definite wall thickening or mass is observed. No masses or enlarged lymph nodes are seen in the abdominopelvic cavity or retroperitoneal area. No effusion is seen in the abdominopelvic cavity.
